title: Swida × dunbarii
intro: Swida × dunbarii, also known as Dunbar's hawthorn or Swida dunbarii, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between S. alba and S. nigra. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European folk medicine, where it is believed to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Swida × dunbarii include flavonoids, anthocyanins, and other phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Swida × dunbarii is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
taxonomy: Swida × dunbarii is a hybrid of the Swida alba and Swida sanguinea. It is a deciduous shrub native to the United States. The classification of Swida × dunbarii is a complex one, as it belongs to the family of Adoxaceae and the genus Swida. It is further classified as a member of the Rosales order and the Angiosperms clade. The hybrid is also known as a shrub with attractive fall foliage.
uses: Swida × dunbarii, also known as the Dunbar's hawthorn, is a hybrid plant used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its berries, leaves, and flowers are used in various treatments, including those for digestive issues and to support cardiovascular health. The plant's antioxidant properties may also help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Traditional remedies use Swida × dunbarii to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and to support the immune system. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

properties:
Swida × dunbarii is known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. 
It is also believed to have antimicrobial effects, which can help protect against infections.

Additionally, Swida × dunbarii is thought to have neuroprotective properties, which may help prevent or slow down neurodegenerative diseases.

constituents: Swida × dunbarii is a hybrid species of the Swida genus, and like other members of the family, it contains various bioactive compounds.
